ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: O karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A biǝ-ba, nɔɔna na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.
translated: The young man said to his father, "Father, I want to go and see the world, because I have heard that there are many beautiful things in it."

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Zezi daa ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na tarɩgɩ kanɩa, oó sɛ o kwe-ka o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ, naa o zɩgɩ-ka gadogo kuri nɩ na? Aye. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ.
translated: “My friend, you are very strong. If you can lift this heavy stone, can you also lift this big log?”

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Nɔɔna zanzan ma bag-o sɩ o pu o ni. O laan ma tiini o kaasɩ zanzan yɩ o wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!»
translated: The woman was carrying a bag of grain on her head. She was going to the market to sell the grain. She said: "This is David's grain, he gave it to me!"

ref: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
text: o na jɩgɩ o karabiǝ bam o brɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O maa brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Baá ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ yɩ ba gʋ-nɩ. Ba nan na gʋ-nɩ, da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a laan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.»
translated: They went to the market to buy some food. The man said: "I saw the Prophet's companions eating a dish made with meat and they said that it is good for health. If you eat it, you will be strong and healthy."

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Fɔgɩ-na á yɩrɩ á tɩtɩ sɩ á taá loori Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ tʋ maŋɩm dɩm maŋa nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ nanzwǝnǝ wʋbʋŋa lagɩ sɩ ya sɛ Baŋa-Wɛ, yɩ o yɩra laan bwǝnǝ.»
translated: The one who is called Fogɩ-na said, "I will go and tell the people of Wɛ that I have seen a great thing. I have seen a very big animal with horns." The people of Baŋa-Wɛ said, "Yes, go and tell us about it."

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Biǝ bam gʋm dɩm ma pa Wɛ nijoŋnu Zeremi na tagɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kalʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.
translated: The people of the village of Wɛ went to Zeremi to ask him to teach them how to make the beautiful cloth.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Kʋ daarɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ nmʋ na wʋ́ da sɩ n vu n joŋi ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn ni yɩ mɩmɩna mʋ, yɩ ka pɩɩnɩ. Nɔɔna fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ.» Ba lwarɩ tiu kʋ biǝ lǝrim nɩ mʋ (Luki
translated: He said to them, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field."

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Ka ma ba ka su lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna bam laan ma vaŋɩ-ka ba tuuri ba ja bɛ. Ba laan ma jǝni tɩga nɩ ba kuri kalǝŋ-ŋwɩ sɩm ba kɩ ba tampwarʋ wʋnɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba kwe sɩlʋ na ba lana tɩn ba dʋlɩ ba yagɩ.
translated: The old man went to the farm. He saw the children playing in the field. The children were playing a game of hide and seek. They were hiding behind the big trees in the forest. They were laughing and having fun.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Ba ma bwɛ daanɩ, yɩ ba ma-kʋ ba yǝgi tɩga kadoŋ, ka yɩrɩ mʋ Kamɔ-mɔrʋ tɩga, yɩ ba ma kɩ vǝrǝ yibeelǝ jǝgǝ.
translated: When they were young, they used to go to the farm, and they used to go to Kamɔ-mɔrʋ to hunt, and they used to go and fish in the river.

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Zezi ma joori o vu sɔŋɔ. Kɔgɔ zanzan daa ta ma ba kʋ gilimi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Kʋ ma pa Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa warɩ ba na laŋa sɩ ba di wʋdiu dɩ.
translated: Jesus was walking along the road. A blind man sat by the roadside begging. Jesus asked him, "What do you want me to do for you?" The blind man said, "Lord, I want to see."

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba yiǝ yam ma sɩɩnɩ ya joori ya puri. O ma kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ.
translated: He went to the market to buy some food for his family. He wanted to buy some meat, some vegetables, and some rice.

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Zan karabiǝ dɛɛn ma vu Zezi te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam yǝni dɩ́ vɔ ni maŋa zanzan. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ nmʋ karabiǝ bam ba vɔ ni kʋntʋ?»
translated: They brought a man possessed by a demon to Jesus, and he was mute. When the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke, and the people were amazed and said, "Never has anything like this been seen in Israel."

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Zezi ma na nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan na durǝ kʋ maa bɩɩnɩ ba te. O laan ma bagɩ cicirǝ kam, yɩ o ta dɩ ka o wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋlʋ na paɩ nɔɔna jiri mɩmaarʋ dɩ zwa-kwarʋ tɩn, a tɛ dɩ nmʋ sɩ n nuŋi bu wʋm yɩra nɩ n viiri, sɩ n daa yɩ joori n yaar-o.»
translated: The old man said to the young men, "I have seen many things in my life, and I know that when a person is in trouble, it is best to be patient and wait for things to get better. Do not be hasty and make a mistake that you will regret later."

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Sɔɔ mʋ zaŋɩ Rama tɩʋ wʋnɩ. Nɔɔna mʋ wʋra ba keerǝ, yɩ ba coosǝ zanzan. Rasɛɛlɩ mʋ tiini o keerǝ o biǝ ŋwaanɩ, o maa ba sɛ sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ gan-o, o biǝ bam na tɩga tɩn ŋwaanɩ.»
translated: The old man said to him: "Go and tell Rama that I am sick. My body is weak, and I cannot walk anymore. Take this medicine to him, and tell him that I need it urgently, because I am very ill."

ref: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
text: o na jɩgɩ o karabiǝ bam o brɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O maa brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Baá ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ yɩ ba gʋ-nɩ. Ba nan na gʋ-nɩ, da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a laan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.»
translated: They said to the Prophet, "We believe in you and we believe in what you have brought from Allah. We will follow you, and we will obey you."

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,

translated: The people of the land said to Jeremiah, "Pray to the Lord your God for us, for we have sinned against him. We have not listened to his voice and have not walked in his ways."

translated: The children went to the river to play. They were swimming and playing in the water. They were catching fish and putting them in a basket. They were happy and enjoying themselves.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.

translated: When the people gathered, they said that they would go to Kamɔ-mɔrʋ to see the beautiful things there. They said they would go and see the beautiful things in Kamɔ-mɔrʋ.

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.

translated: Jesus went to the temple and began to drive out those who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the benches of those who sold doves. And he would not allow anyone to carry merchandise through the temple.
